posts.title: Add EUR Accounts (SEPA)
posts.text:
Add SEPA Instant (SCT Inst) support to nsave for real‑time EUR transfers

Enable customers to send and receive euro payments in seconds, 24/7/365, by integrating SEPA Instant Credit Transfer (SCT Inst). This removes settlement delays and aligns nsave with user expectations for instant wire-like experiences.

Why this matters

	•	Real-time UX: Funds move to beneficiary in ≤10 seconds, typically ~1–2 seconds, with immediate confirmation.

	•	Always-on: Available 24/7/365, unlike traditional SEPA cutoffs and weekend delays.

	•	Competitive parity: Most EU fintechs and major banks are rolling out SCT Inst; EU regulation will mandate instant capability for PSPs offering SEPA credit transfers, with phased timelines.

	•	Fraud resilience: Request-to-Pay and mandatory IBAN/name check (IBAN-Name Check/Confirmation of Payee) reduce misdirection and social engineering risk, while instant rails can be combined with risk throttles.

posts.title: Flexible card shipping options, competitive issuing fees, and Visa debit offering
posts.text:
Card shipping, pricing, and Visa debit offering

	•	Offer tiered shipping:

	•	Economy Untracked: cheapest postal option with clear disclaimers and optional “Shipping Protection.”

	•	Economy Tracked: low-cost tracking.

	•	Standard Courier: tracked with signature.

	•	Express Courier: fastest, premium.

	•	Gate untracked shipping to low‑risk corridors; require KYC completion before dispatch; add address validation to cut returns.

	•	Transparent checkout: show delivery estimates, tracking availability, and full cost breakdown; notify at each step.

	•	Competitive issuing fees:

	•	Keep standard plastic low-cost; virtual cards free/nominal.

	•	Graduated replacement fees; bundle express shipping for premium cards.

	•	Free or subsidized economy shipping above funding/usage thresholds.

	•	Add Visa Debit:

	•	Partner with a BIN sponsor to issue virtual first, then physical.

	•	Support Apple Pay/Google Pay tokenization and 3‑D Secure 2.

	•	Differentiate standard vs premium (e.g., metal, higher limits, perks).

posts.title: Building an Independent Financial Infrastructure for Cards and Accounts
posts.text:
The idea is to build our own card issuing platform instead of relying on third-party partners, whose instability often causes disruptions. By doing this, we would no longer be dependent on partners, which would give us greater stability and reliability of service, avoiding interruptions due to their issues or additional compliance procedures.

The same applies to accounts: currently, a customer might suddenly find their account details (such as IBAN or account address) changed because Nsave stopped working with a specific partner bank. Having our own infrastructure would eliminate such risks.

A good benchmark for this is Wise, which eventually became independent in its financial infrastructure, ensuring customers had a seamless and stable experience.

This should be set as a long-term goal, though we still need to better understand the regulatory requirements involved in card issuing and account management

posts.title: Request for PRC
posts.text:
We request the platform to kindly provide PRC (Payment Realization Certificate) for the payments remitted to our local PKR bank accounts. These PRC's should have 9186 code to correctly reflect the IT Services for proper alignment.

PRC is important because:

-It is the official proof of inward foreign remittance.

-It is required for compliance with the State Bank of Pakistan and other regulators.

-It helps businesses maintain proper records for taxation and audits.

Having timely access to PRCs will make financial reporting and regulatory compliance smoother for all users.

posts.title: residential address
posts.text:
Hello nsave Support Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I am writing to request your assistance regarding the residential address used in my nsave account. I would like to use my real, current residential address to ensure that all account information remains accurate and fully compliant with your verification requirements.

At the moment, I am facing some difficulty updating or confirming my real address through the app/dashboard. I want to make sure that my account details correctly reflect my actual place of residence to avoid any issues in the future, especially related to verification or compliance.

Could you please guide me through the correct process or let me know if any documents are required from my side to update and use my real address successfully?

I would really appreciate your support and guidance on this matter. Thank you for your time and assistance.

Kind regards,

CB

posts.title: Payment Link Fees  and Verification for Freelancers
posts.text:
Hi Amer, thanks for the update! Great to hear Invoicing is coming—that is my #1 feature request as a freelancer.

Feedback on what's missing:

Payment Link Fees: When Payment Links launch, please be transparent about the fees. If it's 3% (like Stripe), it's hard for us. If it's 1% or fixed, we will move all our volume to nsave.

Verification for Freelancers: Onboarding was tough because 'Digital Bank Statements' (like SadaPay/Nayapay) in Pakistan aren't officially listed as accepted Proof of Address, even though they work. Adding them to the official list would help many Pakistani freelancers.

Glad to be an early nsaver!

posts.title: Allow setting the received amount in local currency (EGP)
posts.text:
Currently, the app only allows entering the sending amount in USD, and the received amount in EGP is calculated automatically, often resulting in fractional values.

For users in Egypt, repeated transfers with fractional EGP amounts can raise flags with local banks, as they may be considered suspicious transactions.

It would be very helpful to add an option to manually set the received amount in EGP, and have the app calculate the required USD amount instead. This would make recurring transfers smoother and reduce unnecessary banking issues.

posts.title: Fix Outgoing ACH!
posts.text:
It's been nice following up with the new features coming into nsave, but there are small basic features that are considered a necessity for every USD Bank Account.

I've longly waited for Outgoing ACH transfers to be enabled, once enabled, I finally switched to using nsave.

However, couple days earlier when I attempted a transfer, I got notified that Outgoing ACH was disabled again, seriously?

I basically have my funds locked out in nsave at the moment, I was told to attempt a Wire transfer, and while the application tells me that the transaction would use Wire instead of ACH, every time I attempt the transfer, it returns with a failure, and I have to add that the recipient's details UI is confusing and doesn't help much in this situation, one day it's an ACH transfer, and a Wire in the other... plus it can't even perform the Wire transfer.

I would also like to add that customer support was not very helpful, I was told that I needed an IBAN for the Wire transfer after I just sent them a screenshot of the details I'm attempting to send to (Another virtual US Account, with a VAN, ABA and SWIFT code)

Thank you for every thing, but seriously, this was not what I was expecting.

posts.title: Robust login security with passkey, app lock with biometric for security
posts.text:
Currently nsave is using OTP and no way to add a 2fa key. I will say passkey will make this process very easy. Once the account was created and everything is okay passkey can help future login without OTP hassle. Simply use passkey as a 2nd authorization, even better with full discovery passkeys where user can simply auto fill the authentication without entering email and password first.

Another thing, I will like to have better security and privacy with app lock using android biometric authentication. This way someone can't open the app and transfer anything without original user interaction .

Thank you, I hope the team will consider these needed security measurements and make the app even better.

posts.title: Ability to modify ntag and display name
posts.text: It would be really cool and helpful if you can change your ntag which would be a unique name only given to you. Kinda like how social media usernames work. You would be able to change it to what u think is easy to remember or of your choice and then you cannot change it for 90 days or smthn like that. And a little customization of display names would be awesome like the ability to exclude your middle name or last name. It would be really helpful especially for ppl who have local titles in their names on their IDs (like name of their cast that are only relevant in their country or titles like in my case "hafiz") and don't want them included in the name here as it is supposed to be an international banking acc. Cus keeping these titles on the acc just gives out random information about one self unnecessarily to other ppl which is not ideal for a person managing a buisness or an influencer who prefers to keep their identity hidden valuing their privacy.

posts.text: I'm very unsatisfied with Nsave services. Once I receive payment of 599 GBP from my client because I sold her PS5 Play Station but Nsave hold that money for 13 days and They return my money to clients account. I'm still facing that lose of 599 GBP which returned to my client account whilst in those days my sister was admitted in hospital and I need to help her but they refund that money and put me in lose. I joined all time support team on regular basis and provided them all required every document. I provided each and everything but they rejected my request and holds my money. I never face this bad experience from any bank in UK (Globally). Nsave support team is very to much lazy more than a turtule and they respond me always back when I texted them 20-25 times per day then their bot replied me. Nsave and it's team fucked my account and also put a big loss of 599 GBP for me. I never forget it and I'll put a case from Financial Ombudsman for this behave and I'll put a major case on Nsave department for my loss in soon.

posts.title: Subject Request for Assistance – Failed Amazon Payment to Nsave USD Account
posts.text:
Dear Nsave Support Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I am writing to kindly request your assistance regarding a payment issue related to my USD account with Nsave. I am a seller on Amazon, and I recently experienced a failed royalty payment that was supposed to be deposited into my Nsave account.

Below are the details of the bank account currently connected to my Amazon Merch account:

Bank Name: Lead Bank

Account Number: 215015607761

Routing Number: 101019644

Amazon notified me that the payment transaction was failed. Unfortunately, I have not been able to determine the exact reason for the failure.

I would greatly appreciate it if your team could please review my account and help clarify the following:

Whether my USD account with Nsave is fully active and able to receive ACH payments from Amazon.

If there are any restrictions or issues related to receiving payments from Amazon or via ACH (Automated Clearing House) transfers.

Whether the banking details listed above are correct and properly configured for incoming transfers.

If there are any additional steps or verification required on my side to ensure that future payments are successfully received.

Receiving my Amazon royalties is very important for my business, so I would sincerely appreciate your guidance in resolving this matter as soon as possible.

Thank you very much for your time and support. I look forward to your response.

Kind regards,

Nadji Moralent 

nadjimora00@gmail.com

Support Yemeni Nationals Residing Abroad (e.g. Malaysia)
posts.text:
I am a Yemeni national currently residing in Malaysia as a student with a valid and official residence permit. Many other financial platforms, such as Wise, allow Yemeni nationals who legally reside abroad to open accounts using their passport along with a residency permit and proof of address.

I would really love to see nsave support a similar approach, allowing Yemeni nationals living legally outside Yemen to open accounts based on their country of residence rather than nationality alone. This would help many students and professionals who rely on safe digital financial services.

Thank you for considering this request.

posts.title: Inquiry About Additional Financial Services
posts.text:
I hope this message finds you well.

I am writing to inquire about the availability of additional financial services on your platform. Specifically, I would like to know whether you offer or plan to offer the following features:

Support for receiving and managing funds using cryptocurrencies.

Activation and integration of PayPal as a supported payment method.

The ability to issue unlimited virtual cards for online transactions.

These services would greatly enhance flexibility and convenience for managing online payments and international transactions. I would appreciate it if you could provide detailed information regarding availability, requirements, and any applicable fees.

Thank you for your time and support. I look forward to your response.

Kind regards,

posts.title: Bangladesh payouts being classified as local NPSB instead of remittance
posts.text:
I am a user from Bangladesh and I am facing a serious compliance issue with nsave payouts.

Although my funds originate from my nsave USD account, both my main payout and the 2.5% government incentive were credited to my local bank account via NPSB and treated as local transactions by the receiving bank. As a result, the bank does not recognize these credits as official remittance.

This creates significant risks for users like me:

- Large and recurring “local” NPSB credits can trigger AML or source-of-funds issues.

- Even with valid work proofs or contracts, Bangladeshi banks often rely on transaction classification, not documents.

- This can lead to future tax, compliance, or banking restrictions.

Currently, there is no clear guidance on which payout method ensures consistent remittance recognition for bank accounts in Bangladesh. Direct bank withdrawals appear unreliable, while mobile wallets (e.g., bKash) seem to be treated differently.

I request nsave to please:

1. Clearly document which payout methods are most likely to be recognized as remittance by Bangladeshi banks.

2. Consider improving or clarifying bank payout routing to avoid local NPSB classification where possible.

3. Provide transparency so users can choose payout methods without risking compliance issues.

This is a real and practical problem for Bangladeshi users handling legitimate overseas income. I hope the team will review this seriously.

posts.title: Exchange Notification Text Problem (Doesn't show the End time)
posts.text:
The notification of 'Exchange' shows the start time, but doesn't show the end time.  From Nsave support I learned that it is between 7 AM - 1 PM (GMT)

So, if anyone opens the exchange option at 2 PM GMT (like me) for several days, they will see that, it only works after 1 PM of the next day. But when they open the Exchange option next day at 2 PM GMT, they will see that it will open after 1 PM GMT next day. But as there is no end time in the notification, they will thought '2 PM is after 1 PM, why it is not working?'

That's why, please add the end time in this notification "7 AM to 1 PM"

I am attaching the screenshot from Bangladesh time here (GMT+6). So, don't confuse it with the GMT time I have told above.

posts.title: App UI and Experience Design Improvement
posts.text:
Simplify the overall UI by reducing visual clutter, streamlining navigation, and adopting a cleaner layout style inspired by Wise, N26, or Revolut to make the app more intuitive.

Improve error handling by showing clear, human-readable error messages with actionable suggestions instead of generic alerts.

Optimize performance by reducing app latency, improving loading times, and setting up performance monitoring to quickly address bottlenecks.

Enhance the card section with more useful information such as card spending limits, remaining balances, visual spending charts.

Revamp the support section by moving it to a more prominent position in the app and providing multiple support channels such as FAQs, live chat, and contact forms.

Make onboarding smoother by reducing steps.

posts.title: Local Bank Transfers for Funding Accounts
posts.text: Allow users to fund their nsave accounts through local bank transfers, providing a convenient and familiar method for Lebanese users.

posts.title: Add Direct Local Deposit and Cash-In Options for Lebanon
posts.text: Enable Lebanese users to fund their nsave accounts through licensed exchange houses, cash-in agents, or fintech integrations. This would allow safe conversion of Lebanese pounds or blocked USD into stable currencies without depending on unreliable local banks.

posts.title: Do not block small transfers
posts.text:
I understand that sometimes there are verification checks on transfers, but for transfers ranging from $200 to $600, I don’t see the need for that, as it discourages people from using the app.

Freezing people's funds for a long period is not good and it greatly interferes with their personal interest.

Nevertheless, I still find the app extremely good. Most of the major issues and bugs have been fixed. Its design is fairly good, and it is easy to use. The only remaining concern is the cards, and we hope for improvements in the future. Personally, I opened an account about six months ago, and recently I have been using it more than before.

posts.title: Slice of life improvement
posts.text:
The app doesn't cache previous data. And it doesn't tell if it's loading new data or not. The UI looks weird upon opening the app from a close state due to missing data. At least show us some loading animation. Loading placeholder animation is common to any apps except Nsave 😕.

And I wish the app was more smooth like a good app not to mention as a Fintech app.

Nsave already send the URL in email. I guess the team can just use some markup language to make the invoice and attach it to the email, or better directly render the invoice on the web page. This is not very hard to implement but can improve the experience for both client and receiver.
